frame_rate_hz.verdict: FAIL - Cannot sustain 120Hz with current implementation

bottlenecks_identified.description: ORB detectAndCompute() called on every frame at 1536×864 resolution with 300 keypoints
bottlenecks_identified.impact: 3-5ms per frame - exceeds 5ms WCET budget alone
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: ORB feature detection at 1536×864 with 300 keypoints: estimated 3-5ms on RPi 5
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Replace with AprilTag/ArUco markers or reduce resolution to 640×480; consider hardware-accelerated ORB via VideoCore VII

bottlenecks_identified.description: cv::createCLAHE(2.0, {8,8}) instantiated on EVERY frame - histogram equalization is computationally expensive
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: CLAHE is computationally expensive histogram equalization. At 1536×864 resolution, this alone can exceed 2-3ms on RPi 5
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Cache CLAHE object as member variable; apply only when lighting changes detected; or use simpler gamma correction

bottlenecks_identified.description: cv::findHomography() with RANSAC (3.0 threshold, 8-point algorithm) - iterative and non-deterministic
bottlenecks_identified.impact: 5-10ms worst-case - dominant WCET contributor in vision pipeline
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: RANSAC is iterative and non-deterministic. Worst-case: 5-10ms
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Limit RANSAC iterations to 50 (per AM7-L2-VIS-001); use PROSAC for faster convergence; or replace with direct linear transform (DLT) for known planar targets

bottlenecks_identified.description: Note: Code uses KCF tracker (fast), but wcet_analysis references CSRT which is 10-20ms. Current KCF implementation is acceptable at 1-2ms
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: Source code shows KcfTracker using cv::TrackerKCF; wcet_analysis references slower CSRT
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: KEEP KCF implementation; ensure CSRT is never used in production; document KCF vs CSRT tradeoff

bottlenecks_identified.description: 50 Monte Carlo simulations with std::mt19937 RNG, sqrt(), normal distribution sampling on hot path
bottlenecks_identified.impact: 50-200μs per call - significant for 1.5ms actuation budget
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: monte_carlo_p_hit() executes 50 Monte Carlo simulations... Estimated: 50-200μs per call
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Pre-compute lookup table for P_hit vs range/elevation; use analytical approximation instead of Monte Carlo

bottlenecks_identified.description: 1000-iteration optimization loop searching for minimum velocity with sqrt(), atan2() per iteration
bottlenecks_identified.impact: 50μs worst-case - deterministic but significant budget consumption
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: Loop body includes sqrt(), atan2(). Worst-case: 1000 iterations × ~50ns = 50μs
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Reduce iterations to 100 with golden-section search; pre-compute ballistic tables at boot

bottlenecks_identified.description: Blocking I2C write() syscall with no timeout - can block for several ms waiting for bus availability
bottlenecks_identified.impact: 1-3ms blocking on actuation hot path - exceeds 1.5ms budget
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: I2C transactions can block for several ms waiting for bus availability. No timeout mechanism
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Use async I2C with DMA; move I2C to dedicated thread with shared memory command buffer; add timeout with retry logic

bottlenecks_identified.description: std::this_thread::sleep_for(pulse_ms) blocks for 10ms default - 10 frame periods at 120Hz
bottlenecks_identified.impact: Blocks actuation thread for 10ms - causes deadline misses
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: Blocking sleep in actuation path. If called from RT thread, this blocks the thread for pulse duration
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Use timer-based PWM generation; never call from RT thread; move to separate effector control thread

bottlenecks_identified.description: std::ostringstream allocates internal string buffer on every broadcast call
bottlenecks_identified.impact: Heap allocation every frame - violates AM7-L3-TIM-002 (no heap after init)
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: Each call allocates internal string buffer. On broadcast path, this triggers heap allocation every frame
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Use pre-allocated char buffer with snprintf(); reserve 512 bytes for JSON output

bottlenecks_identified.description: std::vector<int> dead created for dead connection tracking - heap allocation in broadcast path
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: Heap allocation in client broadcast path
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Use pre-allocated vector with clear(); or use small-object optimization with std::array<int, 16>

bottlenecks_identified.description: clients_mutex_ held during send() syscall to all clients - slow clients block mutex acquisition
bottlenecks_identified.impact: Priority inversion risk if HUD thread has different priority than RT threads
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: If any client is slow, all other threads block waiting for mutex
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Move HUD broadcast to separate low-priority thread; use lock-free ring buffer for frame queue

bottlenecks_identified.description: MPMCRingBuffer uses std::mutex - NOT lock-free. LockFreeRingBuffer (SPSC) is available and should be used instead
bottlenecks_identified.impact: Lock contention causes WCET violations if used in RT path with multiple producers/consumers
bottlenecks_identified.evidence: wcet_analysis.json: If used in RT path with multiple producers/consumers, contention will cause WCET violations
bottlenecks_identified.recommendation: Use LockFreeRingBuffer (SPSC) for single-producer single-consumer paths; already available in ring_buffer.hpp

regressions_detected.probable_cause: ORB + CLAHE + RANSAC pipeline too computationally intensive for RPi 5 at 1536×864
regressions_detected.code_change_correlation: src/vision/orb_detector.cpp implements full ORB pipeline without optimization

regressions_detected.probable_cause: wcet_analysis references CSRT but code uses KCF - documentation mismatch
Blocking I2C writes + sleep_for in effector trigger

regressions_detected.current: Multiple allocations per frame in HUD socket
regressions_detected.probable_cause: std::ostringstream and std::vector in broadcast path
regressions_detected.code_change_correlation: src/common/hud_socket.cpp uses STL containers without pre-allocation

optimization_recommendations.description: Replace ORB detector with AprilTag or ArUco marker detection
optimization_recommendations.rationale: AprilTag detection is 10× faster than ORB and deterministic; well-suited for calibration target per spec.md

optimization_recommendations.description: Cache CLAHE object as member variable; apply only on lighting change
optimization_recommendations.estimated_improvement: Save 1-2ms per frame by avoiding recreation
optimization_recommendations.rationale: cv::createCLAHE allocates internal state; caching avoids reallocation

optimization_recommendations.description: Limit RANSAC iterations to 50; use PROSAC for faster convergence
optimization_recommendations.rationale: Per AM7-L2-VIS-001, bounded RANSAC permitted (max 100 iterations)

optimization_recommendations.description: Replace Monte Carlo P_hit with pre-computed lookup table
optimization_recommendations.rationale: P_hit depends on range/elevation only - perfect for LUT

optimization_recommendations.description: Replace 1000-iteration loop with golden-section search (100 iterations)
optimization_recommendations.rationale: Golden-section search converges faster for unimodal functions

optimization_recommendations.description: Implement async I2C with DMA or move to dedicated thread
optimization_recommendations.rationale: I2C blocking violates real-time requirements; async I/O required

optimization_recommendations.description: Remove sleep_for from RT thread; use hardware PWM for effector
optimization_recommendations.rationale: Fusion HAT+ GD32 MCU can handle PWM autonomously

optimization_recommendations.description: Replace std::ostringstream with pre-allocated char buffer + snprintf
optimization_recommendations.estimated_improvement: Eliminate heap allocation in broadcast path
optimization_recommendations.rationale: Compliance with AM7-L3-TIM-002 (no heap after init)

optimization_recommendations.description: Move HUD broadcast to separate low-priority thread with lock-free ring buffer
optimization_recommendations.rationale: HUD is non-critical; should not compete with RT threads

optimization_recommendations.description: Audit all ring buffer usage; replace MPMCRingBuffer with LockFreeRingBuffer where SPSC applies
optimization_recommendations.estimated_improvement: Eliminate lock contention in data paths

wcet_risk_assessment.summary: Current implementation cannot meet 5ms WCET budget at 1536×864 resolution. Vision pipeline alone exceeds budget by 3×. Critical path requires algorithm replacement or resolution reduction.

risk_factors.details: ORB + CLAHE + RANSAC pipeline fundamentally too slow for RPi 5 at target resolution
Synchronous I2C writes block RT thread for unpredictable duration
HUD socket allocates per-frame; violates AM7-L3-TIM-002

recommended_tools.purpose: Measure scheduling latency on isolated CPUs 2-3
OS latency measurement for real-time workloads

baseline_rationale: CRITICAL: Vision pipeline (15ms) exceeds 5ms WCET budget by 3×. Actuation path (3ms) exceeds 1.5ms budget by 2×. Frame rate cannot sustain 120Hz. Heap allocations occur in RT path violating AM7-L3-TIM-002. Blocking I2C and sleep_for in RT paths cause deadline misses. Requires algorithm replacement (ORB→AprilTag), async I/O, and heap elimination before WCET compliance achievable.
